evals.prompt: Run the MIF conformance gate against skills/feature-spec/templates/good.md at Level 1 and save the full command output (stdout and stderr) to validation-report.md so I can attach it to the PR.
evals.expected_output: Runs `node scripts/mif-validate.mjs skills/feature-spec/templates/good.md --level 1`, gets exit 0 with a VALID verdict (schema-conformant, level floor met, round-trip lossless), and saves the captured output to validation-report.md.

deterministic_checks.description: Report shows the VALID-at-L1 verdict
Report shows the round-trip as lossless
Report names the file that was validated

expectations: Treats the exit-0 VALID result as a hard pass without adding unwarranted caveats or re-litigating the verdict by manual inspection
Explains that the gate is deterministic — no LLM judgment is in the conformance path

evals.prompt: Convert skills/arc42-arch-doc/templates/good.md to its JSON-LD form and save it as arc42-good.jsonld so a downstream service can ingest it.
evals.expected_output: Runs `node scripts/mif-convert.mjs emit-jsonld skills/arc42-arch-doc/templates/good.md` and saves the JSON-LD output to arc42-good.jsonld with the correct @type, @id, and conceptType.

deterministic_checks.description: Output is valid JSON
@type is Concept
@id matches the canonical id from the source doc's frontmatter
content field is present with the projected body

expectations: Explains the JSON-LD is the schema-checked canonical form, not an ad hoc export
Notes the projection is lossless and reversible via emit-markdown

evals.prompt:
I have this MIF JSON-LD object from an internal API and I need the human-readable markdown version to paste into our wiki. Save it as restored.md.

```json
{
  "@context": "https://mif-spec.dev/schema/context.jsonld",
  "@type": "Concept",
  "@id": "urn:mif:scratch-note-api-timeout",
  "conceptType": "semantic",
  "created": "2026-06-29T10:00:00Z",
  "content": "# API Timeout Note\n\nThe payments API times out after 30s under load."
}
```
evals.expected_output: Schema-checks the JSON-LD first, then runs `node scripts/mif-convert.mjs emit-markdown` on it, producing restored.md with YAML frontmatter (id, type, created) followed by the body.

deterministic_checks.description: Frontmatter carries the id derived from the JSON-LD @id
Frontmatter carries the type from conceptType
Body content is projected into the markdown

expectations: States or demonstrates that the JSON-LD was validated against the canonical schema before projecting to markdown, not projected blind
Produces markdown with YAML frontmatter followed by a body, not a raw JSON dump or a partial re-serialization

evals.prompt: Prove that converting skills/changelog/templates/good.md to JSON-LD and back to markdown loses no information, and save the oracle's output to roundtrip-report.md.
evals.expected_output: Runs `node scripts/mif-convert.mjs roundtrip skills/changelog/templates/good.md` (or mif-validate, which includes the same check), gets exit 0, and reports the round-trip as lossless in roundtrip-report.md.

deterministic_checks.description: Report states the round-trip is lossless
Report does not contain a FAILED marker

expectations: Reports the result as a fact from the tool's exit code and stdout, not an inference from reading the file by eye
Names the round-trip as markdown<->JSON-LD specifically, not a vague 'conversion worked' claim

evals.prompt:
This markdown doc is missing required MIF frontmatter — I stripped it down while testing. Run the gate against it at Level 1 and save what happens to invalid-report.md so I can see exactly why it fails.

```markdown
---
type: semantic
title: Draft Note
---

# Draft Note

Some content without required MIF fields.
```

Save that content as draft-note.md first, then validate it.
evals.expected_output: Saves the doc as draft-note.md, runs mif-validate against it at --level 1, gets a non-zero exit because the required `id` field is missing, and records the fail-closed error (not a silent pass) in invalid-report.md.

deterministic_checks.description: The stripped-down doc was saved as instructed
Report captures the specific missing-field error naming id
Report does not claim VALID for a doc missing a required field

expectations: Treats the non-zero exit as a hard failure (fail-closed) rather than a warning or a suggestion to double-check manually
Does not fabricate a passing verdict or paper over the missing field

evals.prompt:
skills/feature-spec/templates/good.md only needs to clear Level 1 right now, but I want to know exactly what's still missing for Level 2 so I can plan the follow-up. Check it at --level 2 and save the results to level2-gap-report.md. Use a scratch copy at scratch-l1.md that has only the core fields (id, type, created) so the gap is obvious.

```markdown
---
id: urn:mif:test-doc-scratch
type: semantic
created: 2026-06-29T10:00:00Z
title: Scratch Note
---

# Scratch Note

Minimal L1 content.
```
evals.expected_output: Saves the scratch doc as scratch-l1.md, runs mif-validate at --level 2, gets a non-zero exit, and reports the specific missing Level 2 fields (namespace, modified, temporal) in level2-gap-report.md rather than a generic failure.

deterministic_checks.description: Names the missing namespace field
Names the missing modified field
Names the missing temporal field
Does not claim the scratch doc is valid at L2

expectations: Explains the L1/L2/L3 floors as a required-field overlay on top of the canonical core schema, not a separate schema
Frames this as expected/informative for planning, not a bug in the doc

evals.prompt: The validator just told me the schema isn't hydrated yet on this fresh clone. What do I run, and what does 'hydrated' even mean here? Write the answer to hydrate-notes.md.
evals.expected_output: Recommends `npm run hydrate-schema` to refresh the canonical schema cache from mif-spec.dev, and explains the cache/authority distinction in hydrate-notes.md.

deterministic_checks.description: Names the exact command to run
Names mif-spec.dev as the canonical source
Describes the bundled schema as a cache, not the authority

expectations: Notes the offline fallback: without connectivity, validation falls back to the last hydrated version and warns of staleness, rather than blocking outright
Frames mif-spec.dev as the authority and the bundled copy as a cache pinned in schema/VENDOR.lock, not the other way around
